calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Michael Freedman, Esq. for Jennifer MacKenzie (Administrator) also via Zoom.

The Court stated that it can't find that there's a direct distribution, noted that it was curious as to who would set up a trust to then go around it, stated that there were interesting drafting choices, and that it would take the matter under submission to transfer the assets to the trustee. Mr. Freedman stated that he came in to help save this matter, that he worked on the inventory and appraisal, noted the sale of the securities and the questions raised at the last minute, stated that the will tends to "save" the bad drafting in the first amendment, and that his research is sound. Mr. Freedman also addressed the proceeds of the sale going to the trustee, stated that he is dealing directly with the attorney for the trustee, that the residue is to be distributed, and that they are making the right choices.

At the conclusion of comments, the Second Amended Petition for Final Distribution; Final Report on Waiver of Account of the Administer of the Estate of Keith H. Hopkinson and Petition for Allowance of Compensation to Attorney for Ordinary Services under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cristobal. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
Off Calendar Sep-20-2022 Continued To Dec-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman, via Zoom, for and with Jennifer Mac Kenzie, Personal Representative.

Mr. Freedman reports on the progress that his client has made to obtain bank statements and of working to obtain the necessary, additional statements. Mr. Freedman advises that he has contacted the probate referee to advise him of the preceding and that he hopes to have a corrected inventory and appraisal from the referee soon. The court and Mr. Freedman discuss the matter of the filing of an amended petition which he also intends to file soon.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Dec-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier, via Zoom, for and with Jennifer Mac Kenzie, Administrator.

Counsel reports of filing an amended petition and offers testimony as to the issues which may require her to do so. The court confirms that Mr. Dyer has returned the $6,158 as ordered by the court and is now in this estate's account. The court points to the confusing nature of this petition specifically as to the amount of distribution being requested. Jennifer Mac Kenzie offers testimony regarding the number of accounts and of the distribution amount being requested. The court and counsel review the several Inventory and Appraisals ("I&A") filed in this matter with the court advising that a corrected I&A needs to be filed. The court and counsel review the will and codicil #2 and then confirm what the court is expecting her to accomplish by the next calling of this matter, including, but not limited to, filing an amended petition, filing a corrected I&A, and confirming the amount of distribution to Alicia's estate from the sale of the real property.

The Accounting is continued to Jul-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Filing of Corrected Inventory and Appraisal is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.
